text.title: lessons-learned Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for lessons-learned. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Reject any lesson submission that is missing a field or contains "TBD". Required: title (action-oriented, under 80 chars), project, date, category, impact_level with quantified effect, situation (facts only), root_cause (5-whys), lesson (generalizable, under 2 sentences), recommendation (action verb + owner_role + where_applied), evidence (artifact links).
rule.rationale: Free-text lessons degrade to noise within months; structured fields enforce the information quality required for retrieval and application.

rule.statement: Reject "communication was bad" and any entry where symptom equals cause. Push back with: "go one level deeper — what specifically broke, and why?"
rule.rationale: Vague entries produce vague recommendations; "improve communication" cannot be actioned or measured. A specific mechanism and fix can.

rule.statement: At every project kickoff, retrieve relevant lessons by tag and similarity and insert them into the planning checklist. Track application rate: of last quarter's lessons, what percent triggered a documented process change? Target: above 50%.
rule.rationale: Lessons that are captured but not applied provide zero value; the application rate is the only metric that distinguishes a knowledge system from a documentation exercise.

rule.statement: If application rate falls below 30% for two consecutive quarters, declare the storage or retrieval system broken and redesign the workflow before adding more lessons.
rule.rationale: Adding more lessons to an unused system increases storage cost without benefit; the constraint is retrieval and application, not volume.
